hypothesis: If diffusion-based generative models (CDVAE, GemsDiff, DiffCSP) are trained on preprocessed MP and OQMD datasets, then they will generate novel crystal structures that are chemically valid and thermodynamically stable because DFT-grounded evaluation will show low post-relaxation RMSD, energy above hull ≤0.1 eV/atom (SUN stability), minimum interatomic distances >0.5 Å, and property distributions (density and formation energy) closely matching the training data as measured by EMD, precision/recall, and Fréchet distance.
